bank: Headline with a question format gets more clicks than one without
Headline containing numbers (e.g., '5 ways') gets more clicks than one without numbers
Headline using power words or superlatives gets more clicks than one with neutral words
Headline that is shorter but clear gets more clicks than a longer, wordier headline
Headline with direct address words ('you', 'your') gets more clicks than one without
Headline that evokes stronger emotional words gets more clicks than a neutral headline
Headline that creates a knowledge gap (curiosity) gets more clicks than a straightforward headline
Headline including a time reference ('now', 'today', 'breaking') gets more clicks than one without
Headline that is simpler and easier to read gets more clicks than a complex headline
Headline framed positively gets more clicks than a negatively framed headline in positive news contexts
Headline framed negatively gets more clicks than a positively framed headline in negative news contexts
Headline avoiding clickbait phrasing gets more clicks than a sensationalized headline
Headline with specific details gets more clicks than a vague headline
Headline that implies personal relevance gets more clicks than one that does not
Headline avoiding ambiguity gets more clicks than an ambiguous headline
